title: Idx_cont_training
description: Built an end-to-end MLOps pipeline for continuously training machine learning models on real estate listing data, leveraging a streaming data architecture to keep models up-to-date as new listings entered the market. The pipeline incorporated automated preprocessing steps for handling missing values and outliers, with gradient boosting models (LightGBM, XGBoost) trained and evaluated using scikit-learn, alongside geospatial feature engineering via GeoPandas. The system was designed to reduce model staleness in dynamic property markets by automating the full cycle from data ingestion through model retraining and serialization with Joblib.

title: Portfolio
description: Built a personal portfolio website to showcase data science projects, experience, and resume in a structured, maintainable format. Implemented the site using vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript ES modules with no framework or build step, enabling fast static hosting on GitHub Pages. Automated GitHub repository metadata syncing via custom Node.js scripts that fetch and merge project data into a single source-of-truth data layer without overwriting manually authored content.

title: AI-Driven Resume Quality & ATS Analysis System
description: Built an end-to-end AI hiring analysis system that ingested resumes, LinkedIn exports, GitHub metadata, and interview transcripts into a PostgreSQL knowledge graph, then scored candidate-to-job alignment using a custom ATS engine built on sentence-transformers and scikit-learn. Orchestrated a multi-agent LangGraph pipeline across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama models to generate explainable skill gap analyses and targeted resume improvements, with validation logic that strictly grounded all outputs in existing candidate evidence to prevent hallucinated credentials. Delivered the complete system through an interactive Textual terminal UI, with reproducible ETL pipelines and documented prompt engineering practices producing auditable, evidence-backed hiring recommendations.

title: Diginetica Session Conversion Prediction
description: Built a session-level purchase conversion predictor on the Diginetica CIKM Cup 2016 dataset (308K sessions, ~4% conversion rate), benchmarking XGBoost with flat aggregate features against graph neural network architectures—bipartite GNNs, HGT, and HeteroSAGE—that model item co-view, query–item, and item–category relationships. Implemented strict temporal train/val/test splits to prevent leakage and evaluated all models using AUPRC across multiple seeds to account for class imbalance. Findings quantified whether cross-session graph structure provides meaningful lift over tabular baselines for conversion prediction in sparse e-commerce data.

title: Portfolio Live Editor
description: Built an Express.js proxy server that intercepted requests to a portfolio site and injected a WYSIWYG editing overlay into each served page, enabling live in-browser editing of the site's profile, experience, and project data without touching source files directly. The overlay communicated with a custom REST API that read and wrote changes directly to the source data file, persisting updates instantly with no build pipeline or recompilation required. This eliminated manual file editing during content iteration by centralizing all updates through a point-and-click interface layered over the live site.

title: Real Time ML Feature Store And Quant Research Platform
description: Built a modular quantitative research platform integrating a real-time ML feature store with an event-driven backtesting engine, supporting the full pipeline from daily OHLCV ingestion to tax-loss harvesting signal generation. The system leveraged DuckDB over Parquet for a zero-copy SQL query layer, Feast for feature versioning, and Yahoo Finance as the data source for S&P 500 equity bars with corporate action adjustments. A wash-sale-aware rules engine and a mean-reversion classifier served as the core strategy components, with each pipeline phase independently tested and composable across a six-stage build architecture.

title: California Real Estate Price Prediction
description: Built a machine learning web application that predicts single-family residence prices across California using ensemble models including XGBoost and LightGBM, trained on historical real estate data. Engineered a Streamlit-based interface with interactive visualizations powered by Plotly, enabling users to input property features and receive price estimates in real time. Compared multiple regression approaches using scikit-learn to optimize predictive accuracy, with the final models deployed and served through a responsive, searchable front-end dashboard.

title: eCommerce Session Review Analysis - Predicting Online Shopper Purchase Intent
description: Built classification models using Random Forest and XGBoost on 12,330 e-commerce browsing sessions to predict purchase intent, applying threshold tuning to maximize recall and reduce missed conversions, achieving approximately 90% accuracy. Applied K-Means clustering to segment shoppers into five behavioral archetypes, revealing conversion rates ranging from 0.4% for bounce-prone visitors to 24.3% for high-intent consumers. Identified product browsing depth and administrative page engagement as the strongest purchase predictors using Python, scikit-learn, pandas, and seaborn.

title: Dsc207miniproject
description: Built a data science project using Python to collect, process, and visualize structured datasets through HTTP requests and pandas-based data manipulation. Applied statistical analysis and created visualizations with matplotlib and seaborn to uncover patterns and trends within the data. Demonstrated proficiency in the full data science workflow, from raw data ingestion to insight generation, within a Jupyter Notebook environment.

title: SleeperCel
description: Built a full-stack fantasy football roster optimization tool that integrates with the Sleeper API to fetch real-time league data, rosters, and player availability. The frontend, built with React and Vite, provides an interactive interface for browsing league information, while a Python backend leverages pandas and numpy to power an AI-driven roster building assistant. The system processes live player and roster data to generate actionable lineup recommendations for fantasy football managers.

title: UCSD Research Lab
description: Conducted research at UC San Diego supporting faculty member Andre Gray, applying statistical and computational methods in Jupyter Notebook and R to process and model research datasets. Implemented data pipelines, statistical analyses, and visualizations to extract actionable insights from experimental data. Contributed to ongoing academic research efforts by translating raw data into reproducible, well-documented findings.

title: Bike Traffic in Cambridge Interactive Visualization
description: Built an interactive web visualization of bicycle traffic patterns across Cambridge, Massachusetts using JavaScript, HTML, and CSS. Implemented dynamic filtering and map-based rendering to display bike lane usage and traffic volume data, allowing users to identify high-traffic corridors and underserved cycling infrastructure. The project translated raw municipal traffic datasets into an intuitive, browser-based tool for understanding urban cycling trends.

title: Desktop Tutorial
description: This project served as an introductory exercise in using GitHub Desktop to manage version control workflows. It demonstrated the core Git operations of editing a file, staging changes, and committing updates to a local repository using GitHub Desktop's graphical interface. The exercise established a foundational understanding of README documentation and repository management without requiring command-line interaction.

title: How Airbnb Tourism was impacted by the San Diego Sunshine
description: A static graph which explores Airbnb bookings and revenue throughout the year in San Diego, California. The graph shows how the number of bookings and revenue changes throughout the year, with the highest peaks in the summer months.

title: Study on the Impact of Estrus on Female Rats Throughout the Day
description: This graph is a static visualization of the body temperature across male and female mice. Used to explore differences in the daily cylce of body temperature while highlighting the impact of female estrus.

title: AllRecipes Rating Predictive Classification Model
description: This machine learning model predicts the ratings of recipes on AllRecipes.com based on features of the recipe. We created a multiclass-classifier using a RandomForest.

title: Study of the Presence of Educational Institutions and Income Throughout California
description: An exploratory data analysis of the presence of educational institutions and income throughout California. The project uses a heatmap to show the distribution of educational institutions and income throughout the state of California.

title: Image Processor
description: Built an image processor that can read in an image, apply a filter to it, and then output the processed image. The image processor can apply filters such as negate, grayscale, and edge detection. This project was built using Python.

title: Asteroid Landfall Simulation
description: Written using pandas in the Jupyter Notebook environment, this project simulates the landfall of asteroids using historical datasets, probabilistic modeling, and bootstrapping.

title: Interactive Visualization of Mice Body Temperature Across Gender
description: An interactive visualization of the body temperature across the gender of mice. The visualization allows users to explore the differences in body temperature while allowing filtering and the altering of scope. Features a tooltip which highlights Mouse ID allowing us to examine indvidual mice along with their estrus cycle( should they be female).
